items.title: Why is Visa Inc. (V) One of the Best Stocks to Buy in 2026 for Beginners?
items.description: Visa Inc. (NYSE:V) is one of the best stocks to buy in 2026 for beginners. On January 13, Citi reaffirmed a Buy rating on Visa Inc. (NYSE:V) and set a price target of $450.00. The same day, Bank of America Securities reaffirmed a Buy rating on the stock and set a price target of $382. […]

items.title: Why US retailers want new swipe fee legislation
items.description: US retail groups are urging lawmakers to reform credit card swipe fees, saying dominant networks limit merchant choice.

items.title: 1 Top Dividend Stock to Buy With Double-Digit Dividend Growth and an Aggressive Share Repurchase Program
items.author: Stefon Walters
items.description: American Express is highlighted as a top dividend stock with 17% year-over-year dividend growth and over $25 billion in share repurchases over five years. The company's premium business model, which includes high annual fees, transaction revenue, and interest income, positions it well for long-term growth. With 64% of new accounts from millennials and Gen-Z customers who have 25% more transactions, AMEX demonstrates strong future prospects while maintaining a sustainable dividend payout ratio of only 19% of EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: AMEX demonstrates strong shareholder-friendly policies with 17% annual dividend growth and aggressive $25B+ share repurchase program over five years. The company maintains a sustainable low payout ratio (19% of EPS), has a differentiated premium business model, and is successfully attracting younger demographics with higher transaction volumes, indicating solid long-term growth prospects.

Visa is mentioned only as a competitive comparison point. The article notes that AMEX trails Visa in market position, but this is presented as a strategic choice rather than a weakness, with no specific performance data or sentiment expressed about Visa itself.
Mastercard is mentioned only as a competitive comparison point alongside Visa. No specific performance metrics or sentiment is expressed about the company in the article.
